query: The generated video has a visible, frame‑varying flicker in the film grain overlay, giving it a disjointed look. Which part of the post‑processing stack is introducing this temporal inconsistency?

_tags.author_rationale: The symptom is specific to flicker (temporal artifact) and targets the film grain stage; the agent must isolate whether the grain pattern is being regenerated per frame without a fixed seed or if the sharpening is amplifying noise.
